items.title: Rondje Europa: Ajax wil in Spanje in race blijven voor mirakel
items.summary: Blue Monday is pas net achter de rug of er wordt alweer Europees gespeeld. In Rondje Europa blikken we dinsdag vooruit op de Champions League-clash tussen Villarreal en Ajax.

items.title: Henry looft Brobbey en trekt vergelijking met Premier League-legende Shearer
items.summary: Thierry Henry is lovend over Brian Brobbey. De voormalige ster van Arsenal en Barcelona vindt de spits van Sunderland unieke kwaliteiten hebben. De vertelde de analyticus op de Britse televisie.

items.title: FREE STREAM: Good Morning Transfers

items.summary: Na zes wedstrijden staat Ajax er niet al te florissant voor in de competitiefase van de Champions League. Hoewel een uitschakeling dreigt, is er nog altijd een kleine kans op de tussenronde. Dit is de stand van zaken voor Ajax in het miljardenbal.

items.title: 'You get used to losing!' - Lowry's plan to bounce back from Dubai 'hurt'
items.summary: Shane Lowry believes he can take plenty of learnings into the Hero Dubai Desert Classic after being left 'hurt' by his final-hole disappointment at the Dubai Invitational on Sunday.

items.title: NEC'ers loven Theo Janssen: 'Hij ziet het spelletje'
items.summary: NEC staat dinsdagavond in het KNVB Bekertoernooi tegenover Theo Janssen. De Arnhemmer en Vitesse-icoon is trainer van De Treffers, de tegenstander in de achtste finale. Jasper Cillessen en Dick Schreuder zijn zeer te spreken over de trainer van de Groesbekers.

items.title: Brahim Díaz’s nightmare miss shows dangers of trying to emulate Panenka
items.summary: While the famous penalty technique is the ultimate act of showmanship, the cost of failure is too high to justify Being too smart for your own good is usually drummed out of children before they leave school but sometimes people cannot help themselves. The Panenka penalty, successfully executed, offers the limited benefit of making a goalkeeper look silly and the taker a genius but Brahim Díaz is the latest to learn the cost of what happens when it goes wrong. Díaz was given 15 minutes to consider what to do with his spot-kick after the ludicrous levels of drama in the Africa Cup of Nations final. Maybe this was his undoing: being able to ponder every option, from the rudimentary to the artistic, until deciding to replicate Antonin Panenka’s creation with what could, and should, have been the last kick of the tournament. Continue reading...

items.title: Monaco's Balogun: Playing Real Madrid 'a dream'
items.summary: United States men's national team and Monaco striker Folarin Balogun told ESPN he will achieve one of his dreams on Tuesday when the Ligue 1 club face Real Madrid at the Bernabéu in the Champions League.

items.title: Source: Sporting KC majority stake sold at $700M
items.summary: A minority owner in MLS side Sporting Kansas City has completed a deal to purchase a majority stake in the club from its current owners, a source confirmed to ESPN.

items.title: City confirm $26.8M Guehi signing from Palace
items.summary: Manchester City have announced the signing of England centre-back Marc Guéhi from Crystal Palace on a five-and-a-half-year deal.

items.title: Kostoulas’s brilliant bicycle kick rescues point for Brighton against Bournemouth
items.summary: Just when it seemed that another match would be dominated by the dreariness of a debatable video assistant referee decision, a moment of majesty from Charalampos Kostoulas provided a pertinent reminder of the beauty that football can provide. With his side staring at what would have been a controversial defeat, the 18-year-old Kostoulas found himself facing away from the Bournemouth goal near the penalty spot when the ball bounced towards him in the first minute of injury time. A touch on his chest bought time to set himself, before a wonderful bicycle kick sent the Amex Stadium wild. Continue reading...
